summary: Frontend-only landing page testing for Perfect RMC. All 17 review requirements verified via Playwright on desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844). Page loads at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with no console errors. Hero, Services (6 cards), Process (5 steps), Stats counters (13+/500+/24/7/100%), WhyUs, Clients marquee, Fleet (TATA 7 Cum + 5 specs), WhatsApp CTA, Footer (3 phones / 2 emails / Jarod-Vadodara address / hours) all render with correct content. All WhatsApp CTAs point to https://wa.me/918255009999. Animated concrete background, smooth scroll for nav links, and mobile menu toggle all work. data-testid attributes are present on every interactive element listed in the request.

ui_bugs.issue: Floating button remains visible after programmatically scrolling back to top (window.scrollTo(0,0)). The scroll handler should toggle off when scrollY <= 500. May be a scroll-event/animation timing issue; in normal manual scroll it likely hides, but worth verifying initial state on mount (currently defaults to false, which is fine).

issues: Open mobile menu uses bg-black/95 + backdrop-blur but the hero heading text still bleeds through visually behind the menu items. Consider solid bg-[#050505] or full-screen overlay for cleaner stacking.

action_items: (Low) Investigate FloatingWhatsApp re-hide behaviour when user scrolls back above 500px; consider also setting `show=false` on a resize/route change. Real-user manual scroll likely works; tested via scrollTo programmatically.
(Optional) Use solid background on the mobile nav drawer so the hero text doesn't show through.
critical_code_review_comments: ConcreteBackground deterministic particles (22) — good, avoids hydration mismatch.
All WhatsApp CTAs correctly use whatsappLink() helper -> single source of truth for the number 918255009999. Good DRY.
All sections use semantic ids (#services, #process, #why, #contact) matching navbar links. Smooth scroll enabled in App.js.
No backend code present — matches requirement (pure frontend landing page).
data-testid coverage on every interactive element and informational block — excellent testability.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth on this site
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure marketing landing page, no backend. All 6 services, 5 process steps, 4 stats, 4 why-us points, 10 client logos, 5 fleet specs render. WhatsApp number = 918255009999 used everywhere via whatsappLink() in /app/frontend/src/lib/company.js. Re-test only if FloatingWhatsApp hide-on-scroll-up is fixed or mobile menu background is changed.
rca of the issue: FloatingWhatsApp: setShow is updated only inside the scroll listener. When window.scrollTo(0,0) is fired via JS, a scroll event does fire and should call setShow(false). Possibly the AnimatePresence exit animation (~300ms) or React batching delayed the DOM update during the 800ms wait. Likely not a real user-impacting bug — flagged as LOW.
